pattern: With three trucks for two logs, at most one over-long log needs a single cut, and cutting it exactly at the truck limit minimizes the product cost.
hints: A log already at most k needs no cut and costs nothing.
Because each length is at most 2k, an over-long log only ever needs one cut into two truck-legal pieces.
Cut an over-long log of length x into k and x-k so both pieces fit, paying k*(x-k); sum this over the at-most-one log that exceeds k.

edge_inputs.description: both logs at the limit exactly
both logs exceed the limit, two cuts
one just over, one just under

stress.description: random valid lengths within 2k of the limit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(2, 100000)
    n = rng.randint(1, 2 * k)
    m = rng.randint(1, 2 * k)
    return [n, m, k]
stress.note: each length is at most 2k so at most one cut per log
